understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by moving the selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (if there are multiple '+' tiles, all must be covered simultaneously to trigger WIN).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Actions were done by CLICKing grid coordinates. A piece had to be selected (typically by clicking on it), and then clicking another tile attempted to move the selected piece toward/into that target location. Movement was constrained by walls/obstacles and other pieces: if the path/landing was blocked, the piece would not move (as seen when a CLICK produced 'blocked or no movement effect'). When movement was possible, the selected piece would relocate accordingly. Steps were limited per level/run, so wasted clicks reduced the remaining budget.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by moving the selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (if there are multiple '+' tiles, all must be covered simultaneously to trigger WIN).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Actions were done by CLICKing grid coordinates. A piece had to be selected (typically by clicking on it), and then clicking another tile attempted to move the selected piece toward/into that target location. Movement was constrained by walls/obstacles and other pieces: if the path/landing was blocked, the piece would not move (as seen when a CLICK produced 'blocked or no movement effect'). When movement was possible, the selected piece would relocate accordingly. Steps were limited per level/run, so wasted clicks reduced the remaining budget.
